criteria: The agent has read Section 2.1 completely to understand the RoPE preliminaries, including how the relative position matrix M is constructed as a Toeplitz matrix.

The agent has read Section 2.2 completely to understand the three guiding principles of AdaGroPE: minimizing reuse, prioritizing distant relative position reuse, and progressively increasing reuse count from close to distant.
The agent has read Section 2.3 completely to understand the adaptive relative position adjustment strategy and the general closed-form formula for M_a[i][j].

The agent has read Section 3 completely to understand the experimental setup including all models (Llama-2 7b/13b, Llama-3 8b, Mistral 7b, SOLAR 10.7b, Phi-2), baselines (DCA, SelfExtend, Longlora, Together, CodeLlama, CLEX), and datasets (PG19, passkey retrieval, LongBench, L-Eval).
The agent has read Table 2 to understand the perplexity (PPL) evaluation results on PG19 across context lengths 4k, 8k, 16k, 32k.
The agent has read Figure 2 to understand the passkey retrieval accuracy results for Mistral-7b with different methods across context lengths and depths.
The agent has read Table 3 to understand the LongBench evaluation results across 16 subtasks for all models.
The agent has read Table 4 to understand the L-Eval evaluation results on 4 closed-ended tasks.
The agent has read Figure 3 to understand how AdaGroPE performance scales as context length increases for passkeys of 16, 48, and 64 digits.
The agent has read Figure 4 to understand the ablation study on the selection of P and r using Qasper and Lcc from LongBench.
The agent has read Figure 5 to understand the varying-length passkey retrieval experiment comparing fine-tuned and training-free methods on Llama-2-chat-7b.

The plan includes implementing the core AdaGroPE relative position matrix M_a[i][j] following all three guiding principles: minimizing reuse, prioritizing distant position reuse, and progressively increasing reuse count.
The plan includes computing the L_n^max sequence and deriving G_s^m(L) to determine the maximum reuse count for any given target extension length L.
The plan includes implementing the r_n sequence that defines the minimum number of relative positions retained with each reuse count when G_s^m exceeds that count.
The plan includes implementing both mapping functions f_n^m(x) and f_n^r(x) for remapping original relative positions to AdaGroPE-adjusted positions.
The plan includes integrating AdaGroPE into the LLM self-attention mechanism as a plug-and-play, training-free replacement of standard RoPE relative positions.
The plan includes loading and testing all model variants: Llama-2 (7b and 13b), Llama-3 (8b), Mistral (7b), SOLAR (10.7b), and Phi-2.
The plan includes implementing DCA (Dual Chunk Attention) and SelfExtend as training-free baselines for comparison.
The plan includes language modeling evaluation on PG19 with context lengths 4096, 8192, 16384, and 32768 tokens.
The plan includes passkey retrieval evaluation with a 36-digit passkey across context lengths from 8k to 64k.
The plan includes evaluation on LongBench across all 16 subtasks.
The plan includes evaluation on L-Eval's 4 closed-ended tasks: TOFEL, QUALITY, Coursera, and SFiction.
The plan includes an ablation study varying P values and r values on Qasper and Lcc from LongBench with Llama-2-7b-chat.
The plan includes varying-length passkey retrieval experiments with digit lengths {5, 8, 16, 36, 48, 64, 100} and the context-length scaling analysis with passkey lengths {16, 48, 64}.
The neighbor window of size w_n = rP is implemented to preserve exact original relative positions i-j for all tokens with relative distance i-j < w_n (Eq. 4).
The L_n^max sequence is computed according to the general formula L_n^max = nP - sum_{k=1}^{n-1}(n-k-1)*r_k (Eq. 15), with the base cases L_1^max = P, L_2^max = 2P - w_n (Eq. 5).
The maximum reuse count G_s^m(L) is correctly computed for any given input length L by comparing against the L_n^max thresholds (Eq. 16).
The d_n value is computed as d_n = L - L_n^max for L in (L_n^max, L_{n+1}^max], representing the number of relative positions that must be reused n+1 times (Eq. 6).
The r_n sequence is implemented according to Eq. 9: r_n = floor(r/n * P) when log2(n) is a non-negative integer, and r_n = 0 otherwise, with r_1 = w_n.
The f_n^m(x) mapping function is implemented as L - (n+1)*d_n + floor((x - (L - (n+1)*d_n)) / (n+1)), mapping the farthest positions to their maximum-reuse-count relative positions (Eq. 8).
The f_n^r(x) mapping function is implemented inductively as sum_{k=1}^{n-1} r_k*k + floor((x - sum_{k=1}^{n-1} r_k*k) / n), mapping positions with reuse count up to n (Eq. 12).
The complete general formula for M_a[i][j] (Eq. 17) is correctly implemented, selecting among f_1^r, f_2^r, ..., f_{g_l}^r, f_{g_l}^m based on the value of i-j, where g_l = G_s^m(L) - 1.
The default hyperparameter r is set to 0.25 and the neighbor window size w_n is derived as w_n = rP.
The position mapping dynamically adapts to the actual input sequence length L at inference time, ensuring optimal performance for variable-length inputs.
AdaGroPE is integrated into the LLM self-attention by replacing the standard RoPE relative position indices with the M_a[i][j] values when computing query-key dot products.
The implementation correctly handles the boundary condition where L <= P (G_s^m = 1), in which case no reuse is applied and original relative positions are preserved.
Algorithm 1 (pseudocode for computing relative positions during decoding, provided in the Appendix) is faithfully implemented in code.
Llama-2 7b and 13b models are loaded with AdaGroPE applied to their self-attention layers for evaluation.
Llama-3 8b, Mistral 7b, SOLAR 10.7b, and Phi-2 models are loaded with AdaGroPE applied for evaluation.
The DCA (Dual Chunk Attention / ChunkLlama) baseline is implemented and applied to the same set of models.
The SelfExtend baseline is implemented and applied to the same set of models.
Perplexity (PPL) evaluation on the PG19 validation set is implemented with context lengths 4096, 8192, 16384, and 32768 tokens.
Fine-tuned long-context models (Longlora-7b-32k, Together-7b-32k, CodeLlama-7b-16k, CLEX-7b-16k) are included as additional baselines in the language modeling PPL experiment.
The passkey retrieval task is set up with a 36-digit passkey randomly placed within a 400-token span, tested at multiple depths within the document.
Passkey retrieval is tested across context lengths from 8k to 64k tokens, with 10 evaluation iterations per (context length, depth) combination.

LongBench evaluation is implemented covering all 16 subtasks: NarrativeQA, Qasper, MultiField-en, HotpotQA, 2WikiMQA, Msique, GovReport, QMSum, MultiNews, TREC, TriviaQA, SAMSum, PassageCount, PassageRe, Lcc, and RepoBench-P.
L-Eval evaluation is implemented on 4 closed-ended tasks: TOFEL (3k-5k tokens), QUALITY (4k-9k tokens), Coursera (5k-17k tokens), and SFiction (6k-27k tokens).
The ablation study is implemented by varying P across {1024, 2048, 4096} and r^{-1} across {2, 3, 4, 5, 8} on the Qasper and Lcc datasets from LongBench using Llama-2-7b-chat.

The varying-length passkey retrieval experiment is implemented with passkey digit lengths {5, 8, 16, 36, 48, 64, 100} comparing fine-tuned models (Vicuna-1.5-7b-16k, Llama-2-7b-32k, Longlora) against training-free methods using Llama-2-chat-7b.
The context length scaling experiment is implemented with passkey lengths {16, 48, 64} digits across context window sizes {8k, 16k, 32k, 64k, 128k} for ChunkMistral, SE-Mistral, and AdaGroPE-Mistral.
Language modeling experiments (PPL on PG19) run successfully for all models and context lengths without errors.
Passkey retrieval experiments (36-digit passkey, 8k-64k context) execute successfully for all models, depths, and context lengths without errors.
LongBench evaluation experiments run successfully for all models and all 16 subtasks without errors.
L-Eval experiments run successfully for all 4 closed-ended tasks and all relevant models without errors.
Ablation study experiments on the selection of P and r execute successfully without errors.
Varying-length passkey retrieval and context length scaling analysis experiments execute successfully without errors.
PPL results match Table 2: AdaGroPE-Llama-2-7b achieves perplexity of 7.87/7.65/7.56/7.75 at 4k/8k/16k/32k context lengths.
PPL results match Table 2: AdaGroPE-Llama-3-8b achieves perplexity of 9.04/8.71/8.57/8.52 at 4k/8k/16k/32k context lengths.
PPL results match Table 2: AdaGroPE outperforms or matches SelfExtend across context lengths for both Llama-2-7b and Llama-3-8b models.
PPL results match Table 2: Training-free AdaGroPE outperforms fine-tuned models (Longlora-7b-32k, Together-7b-32k) on PPL at 16k and 32k context lengths.
Passkey retrieval results match Figure 2: AdaGroPE with Mistral-7b achieves nearly 100% passkey retrieval accuracy across all depths and context lengths (8k to 64k).

LongBench results match Table 3: AdaGroPE achieves the best average score compared to DCA and SelfExtend for each model group (Llama-2-7b-chat avg 35.27, Llama-3-8b-ins avg 47.10, Mistral-7b-ins avg 40.77, SOLAR-10.7b-ins avg 42.62, Phi-2 avg 26.15).
L-Eval results match Table 4: AdaGroPE-Llama-2-7b-chat achieves avg 49.28, outperforming SE-Llama-2-7b-chat (47.51) and Longchat-1.5-7b-32k (41.85).
L-Eval results match Table 4: AdaGroPE-SOLAR-10.7b-ins achieves avg 69.83, outperforming SE-SOLAR-10.7b-ins (68.34) on all 4 closed-ended tasks.

Context length scaling results match Figure 3: AdaGroPE-Mistral maintains approximately 95% accuracy at 128k context with a 64-digit passkey, while ChunkMistral drops to ~60% and SE-Mistral drops to ~60%.

Ablation results match Figure 4: The selection of P has a more significant impact on AdaGroPE performance than r; the default r=0.25 (r^{-1}=4) consistently yields strong average performance across Qasper and Lcc.

Varying-length passkey results match Figure 5: AdaGroPE-Llama-2-7b-chat shows milder performance degradation than baselines as passkey length increases, maintaining ~95% accuracy even at 100-digit passkeys while Vicuna-1.5-7b-16k and Longlora drop below 65%.
